ueVirtual.hero.title: UE Virtual
ueVirtual.hero.desc: A virtual studio system that combines virtual scenes, real-time rendering, subject blending, tracking control, and production workflow in one structure, built for teams that need stable visual quality and repeatable production processes.
ueVirtual.positioning.title: This is not just a basic green screen setup
ueVirtual.positioning.desc1: UE Virtual is not only about pulling a subject out of a green screen. It combines virtual scenes, switching, graphics packaging, tracking movement, and production workflow into a system that can actually be used in real content production.
ueVirtual.positioning.desc2: For customers, the difference is not whether the visuals look flashy, but whether the system can become part of daily production and support corporate live streaming, program recording, online courses, and branded content in a stable way.
ueVirtual.differences.title: How is it different from a basic green screen or simple live streaming setup?
ueVirtual.differences.items.0.title: More than background replacement
ueVirtual.differences.items.0.desc: Basic green screen workflows usually stop at keying and background compositing. UE Virtual focuses on the consistency between subject, scene, lighting, and overall production flow.
ueVirtual.differences.items.1.title: More than a single visual effect
ueVirtual.differences.items.1.desc: It is not limited to one virtual scene output. It can bring scene switching, camera motion, graphic packaging, and directing workflow into one production logic.
ueVirtual.differences.items.2.title: More than a technical demo
ueVirtual.differences.items.2.desc: The value of this system is not simply to showcase a visually impressive studio, but to reliably support real production and program execution over time.
ueVirtual.differences.items.3.title: More than software — it is a production framework
ueVirtual.differences.items.3.desc: From rendering and keying to tracking and workflow control, UE Virtual works more like a complete production capability than a standalone tool.
ueVirtual.customerProblems.title: If these situations sound familiar, UE Virtual becomes more valuable
ueVirtual.customerProblems.items.0.title: Fragmented systems increase on-site coordination cost
ueVirtual.customerProblems.items.0.desc: When switching, graphics, packaging, tracking, and virtual scenes are spread across different devices and interfaces, communication becomes harder and mistakes become more likely.
ueVirtual.customerProblems.items.1.title: Green screen works, but the output still does not look like a finished program
ueVirtual.customerProblems.items.1.desc: Hair edges, shadows, color spill, and environmental lighting often remain unconvincing, making the result feel like a test rather than a production-ready output.
ueVirtual.customerProblems.items.2.title: Content output grows, but the workflow is hard to repeat
ueVirtual.customerProblems.items.2.desc: When projects move from one-off shoots to recurring programs, long-term courses, or regular business content, workflow standardization becomes more important than a one-time visual effect.
ueVirtual.value.title: The value customers can actually feel
ueVirtual.value.desc: Customers do not buy technical terminology. They buy whether the system can produce more stable results.
ueVirtual.value.items.0.title: More consistent visual quality
ueVirtual.value.items.0.desc: Virtual scenes become more than backgrounds and move closer to the visual standard expected in professional programs and branded content.
ueVirtual.value.items.1.title: A more centralized workflow
ueVirtual.value.items.1.desc: Multiple production steps are brought into a clearer operating logic, reducing switching friction and coordination complexity on site.
ueVirtual.value.items.2.title: More natural blending between subject and scene
ueVirtual.value.items.2.desc: Improves hair detail, shadows, color spill, and overall lighting match, helping the result feel closer to production-ready output.
ueVirtual.value.items.3.title: Easier to scale and standardize
ueVirtual.value.items.3.desc: As you need more scenes, more show formats, or a more regular production rhythm, the system becomes easier to extend.
ueVirtual.capabilities.title: Core technical capabilities
ueVirtual.capabilities.desc: The value of these capabilities is not simply that there are many of them, but that together they support a more complete virtual production workflow.
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.0.title: PBR Real-Time Rendering
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.0.desc: Processes lighting, color, materials, and scene presentation in real time with physically based rendering, supporting PBR materials, ray tracing, water systems, sky, environmental lighting, and particle effects.
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.1.title: Unified Production Control
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.1.desc: Combines switching, graphics, packaging, camera control, and lighting control into one interface, reducing coordination cost across multiple systems.
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.2.title: Timeline-Based Workflow Control
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.2.desc: Places camera movement, graphics, packaging, motion, and switching points into one workflow logic, making production easier to manage and repeat.
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.3.title: 3D Ray-Traced Chroma Key
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.3.desc: Uses real-time 3D green box modeling and dynamic lighting capture to preserve hair, shadows, and edge detail while improving color recovery and tonal integration.
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.4.title: Smart Tracking and Stabilization
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.4.desc: Combines image recognition, pose estimation, and multiple tracking methods to strengthen virtual camera motion and overall shot stability.
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.5.title: 4K Smart Reframing and Dual-Channel Output
ueVirtual.capabilities.items.5.desc: Uses high-resolution input to generate more close-up and wide-motion possibilities while supporting preview and output workflows needed in real productions.
ueVirtual.useCases.title: What kinds of content is it suitable for?
ueVirtual.useCases.items.0.title: Corporate Live Streaming
ueVirtual.useCases.items.0.desc: Suitable for product launches, press events, briefings, and business content that need a stronger visual standard.
ueVirtual.useCases.items.1.title: Program Recording
ueVirtual.useCases.items.1.desc: Suitable for news-style productions, interviews, forums, feature content, and multi-segment shows that need flexible scene control.
ueVirtual.useCases.items.2.title: Online Courses and Educational Content
ueVirtual.useCases.items.2.desc: Helps combine speakers, teaching materials, charts, and virtual scenes into a consistent educational presentation.
ueVirtual.useCases.items.3.title: Branded and Product Content
ueVirtual.useCases.items.3.desc: Suitable for branded videos, product explainers, and marketing content that need stronger packaging and visual identity.
ueVirtual.integration.title: Compatible modules
ueVirtual.integration.desc: UE Virtual works better as part of a complete production workflow rather than as a standalone system. When combined with other modules, it can turn virtual scenes into more stable content output.
ueVirtual.integration.items.0.desc: Integrates switching and show control so virtual scenes and production flow stay aligned.
ueVirtual.integration.items.1.desc: Adds lower thirds, information bars, and graphic packaging to complete the screen presentation.
ueVirtual.integration.items.2.desc: Supports presenter and host delivery workflows for more stable recording.
ueVirtual.integration.items.3.desc: Helps organize signal conversion and routing for better overall compatibility.
ueVirtual.implementation.title: Implementation process
ueVirtual.implementation.items.0.title: Requirement Review
ueVirtual.implementation.items.0.desc: Confirm content type, production goals, space conditions, and budget direction first.
ueVirtual.implementation.items.1.title: Module Planning
ueVirtual.implementation.items.1.desc: Plan virtual scenes, switching workflow, graphic packaging, and whether additional modules should be integrated.
ueVirtual.implementation.items.2.title: System Testing
ueVirtual.implementation.items.2.desc: Test scenes, keying, tracking, and workflow stability before launch.
ueVirtual.implementation.items.3.title: Delivery and Expansion
ueVirtual.implementation.items.3.desc: After deployment, the system can continue to expand according to future show formats and content needs.
ueVirtual.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
ueVirtual.faq.items.0.q: Do I need to introduce the whole system at once?
ueVirtual.faq.items.0.a: Not necessarily. The setup can be planned based on your budget, space conditions, and content type.
ueVirtual.faq.items.1.q: Is it suitable for both corporate live streaming and program recording?
ueVirtual.faq.items.1.a: Yes. The same system can support corporate live streaming, program recording, online courses, and branded content, with the setup adjusted to the actual use case.
ueVirtual.faq.items.2.q: Can it work with existing equipment?
ueVirtual.faq.items.2.a: Yes. In most projects, the system is planned around site conditions and existing equipment rather than requiring a complete replacement.
ueVirtual.faq.items.3.q: Is there room for customized scenes and visual style?
ueVirtual.faq.items.3.a: Yes. Virtual scenes and presentation style can be planned according to show format, brand style, and production needs.
ueVirtual.cta.title: Want to see whether UE Virtual fits your production workflow?
ueVirtual.cta.desc: If you are planning a virtual studio, corporate live production, program recording, or a more complete virtual production setup, we can help configure the right structure based on your content type, space conditions, and budget.
uShow.hero.title: U-Show
uShow.hero.desc: A control system that brings source inputs, media playback, graphics, switching, live streaming, and local recording into one structured broadcast workflow, built for teams that need stable output and clear operating logic.
uShow.positioning.title: The control core on the broadcast side, not just a switcher
uShow.positioning.desc1: U-Show is not only about switching shots. It organizes preview, program output, media, graphics, split layouts, live streaming, and recording into one broadcast logic.
uShow.positioning.desc2: For customers, the value is not about having more features. It is about whether on-site signals, program flow, and broadcast control can be organized into a more stable and manageable workflow.
uShow.differences.title: How is it different from basic streaming software or simple switching workflows?
uShow.differences.items.0.title: More than a single output feed
uShow.differences.items.0.desc: U-Show uses formal PVW and PGM logic so preview and final output are clearly separated, instead of making live output decisions without a proper preview structure.
uShow.differences.items.1.title: More than camera input
uShow.differences.items.1.desc: In addition to external sources, it can integrate video, images, PPT, external subtitle systems, and CG preview to support fuller program workflows.
uShow.differences.items.2.title: More than live streaming
uShow.differences.items.2.desc: It considers both live output and local recording, so the content is not only streamed out but also kept for editing, backup, and reuse.
uShow.differences.items.3.title: More than switching — it is show flow control
uShow.differences.items.3.desc: Transitions, graphics, split-screen layouts, media playback, and on-air monitoring are all brought into one operating logic, making the result closer to a complete production process.
uShow.customerProblems.title: If these situations sound familiar, U-Show becomes more valuable
uShow.customerProblems.items.0.title: There are many sources on site, but the integration is messy
uShow.customerProblems.items.0.desc: Cameras, presentations, video clips, graphic cards, and subtitles sit in different systems, which can easily disrupt the production flow during switching.
uShow.customerProblems.items.1.title: You need both live output and recording, but the workflow becomes fragile
uShow.customerProblems.items.1.desc: When content needs to be streamed out, archived internally, and prepared for later editing at the same time, a poorly integrated workflow quickly turns chaotic.
uShow.customerProblems.items.2.title: You want a show-style result, but the control flow is not centralized enough
uShow.customerProblems.items.2.desc: When transitions, graphics, split layouts, and media playback are handled separately, it becomes much harder to produce a stable and well-paced result.
uShow.value.title: The value customers can actually feel
uShow.value.desc: For customers, the value of a broadcast control system is not how many features it lists, but whether the production becomes more stable, clearer, and easier to execute.
uShow.value.items.0.title: A clearer broadcast workflow
uShow.value.items.0.desc: Preview, final output, media switching, and monitoring are organized more clearly, reducing confusion on site.
uShow.value.items.1.title: Easier multi-source integration
uShow.value.items.1.desc: Cameras, videos, images, PPT, graphics, and external sources can enter the workflow in a more consistent way.
uShow.value.items.2.title: Live output and recording can be handled together
uShow.value.items.2.desc: Supports both external streaming and local recording, making archiving, backup, and secondary use easier.
uShow.value.items.3.title: A stronger show-style presentation
uShow.value.items.3.desc: With transitions, graphics, split-screen layouts, and media arrangement, it becomes easier to build rhythm and packaging into the output.
uShow.capabilities.title: Core functional capabilities
uShow.capabilities.desc: U-Show is not built as a collection of disconnected features. Its functions are designed around broadcast control and show flow.
uShow.capabilities.items.0.title: PVW / PGM Broadcast Logic
uShow.capabilities.items.0.desc: Provides both preview and program output views so switching decisions can be made with more confidence.
uShow.capabilities.items.1.title: Multi-Source Integration
uShow.capabilities.items.1.desc: Supports management of external signal sources, video, images, PPT, external subtitle systems, and CG preview.
uShow.capabilities.items.2.title: Live Streaming and Local Recording
uShow.capabilities.items.2.desc: Supports RTMP / RTMPS streaming and built-in local recording so live output and recording can run together.
uShow.capabilities.items.3.title: Transition Effects
uShow.capabilities.items.3.desc: Provides transition effects during output switching, helping the result feel more complete than simple hard cuts.
uShow.capabilities.items.4.title: Graphics and CG Packaging
uShow.capabilities.items.4.desc: Supports lower-third bar design, 3D-style graphics treatment, and coordination with CG preview and on-air control.
uShow.capabilities.items.5.title: Split-Screen Layout and Composition
uShow.capabilities.items.5.desc: Allows multiple source feeds to be arranged into custom layouts, suitable for interviews, forums, teaching, and presentation-based content.
uShow.useCases.title: What kinds of content is it suitable for?
uShow.useCases.items.0.title: Corporate Live Streaming
uShow.useCases.items.0.desc: Suitable for launches, internal training, press events, and business-focused live content.
uShow.useCases.items.1.title: Forums and Interviews
uShow.useCases.items.1.desc: Suitable for talk-based content that needs multiple views, graphics, transitions, and split layouts.
uShow.useCases.items.2.title: Online Courses
uShow.useCases.items.2.desc: Can combine speakers, teaching materials, PPT, videos, and graphics into a more stable instructional workflow.
uShow.useCases.items.3.title: Program Recording
uShow.useCases.items.3.desc: Suitable for production formats that need fixed output logic, media switching, and recording retention.
uShow.integration.title: Compatible modules
uShow.integration.desc: As the broadcast control core, U-Show works well with other modules so content is not only created, but also delivered more reliably.
uShow.integration.items.0.desc: Brings virtual scene and real-time rendered output into the switching and broadcast workflow.
uShow.integration.items.1.desc: Adds lower thirds, information bars, and graphic packaging for a more complete visual presentation.
uShow.integration.items.2.desc: Supports presenter and host delivery workflows for smoother live and recorded sessions.
uShow.integration.items.3.desc: Helps organize different devices and signal routes for better overall compatibility and stability.

uShow.implementation.items.0.desc: Start by confirming content type, number of sources, output needs, and whether streaming or recording is required.
uShow.implementation.items.1.title: Broadcast Workflow Planning
uShow.implementation.items.1.desc: Plan source inputs, media playback, graphics, split-screen layouts, and transition logic according to project needs.
uShow.implementation.items.2.title: Testing and Integration
uShow.implementation.items.2.desc: Confirm that output, streaming, recording, and media switching run reliably in the actual workflow.
uShow.implementation.items.3.title: Delivery and Launch
uShow.implementation.items.3.desc: After deployment, the setup can continue to expand and adapt as future program formats and content needs evolve.
uShow.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
uShow.faq.items.0.q: Can it be used only for live streaming?
uShow.faq.items.0.a: Yes, but its value goes beyond streaming. It also includes show control, source integration, graphics handling, and local recording workflow.
uShow.faq.items.1.q: Can it stream and record at the same time?
uShow.faq.items.1.a: Yes. U-Show supports live streaming and local recording together, making simultaneous output and archiving possible.
uShow.faq.items.2.q: Can it play video, images, or PPT?
uShow.faq.items.2.a: Yes. The system can integrate images, video, PPT, and other media sources, making it suitable for business presentations, teaching, and show content.
uShow.faq.items.3.q: Is it suitable for both corporate content and program-style production?
uShow.faq.items.3.a: Yes. From corporate live streaming to interviews, forums, courses, and recorded programs, the setup can be adjusted to fit the format.
uShow.cta.title: Want to see whether U-Show fits your broadcast workflow?
uShow.cta.desc: If you are planning corporate live streaming, forums, interviews, online courses, or recorded programs, we can help configure a more suitable control structure based on your content type and output needs.
uCg.hero.title: U-CG
uCg.hero.desc: A 4K graphics system that combines title graphics, show packaging, template management, Fill / Key output, and broadcast workflow in one structure, built for environments that need reliable on-air graphics, faster updates, and long-term template reuse.
uCg.positioning.title: More than adding text — a complete graphics packaging system
uCg.positioning.desc1: U-CG is not just about placing text on screen. It brings titles, information bars, scoreboards, show graphics, template management, and broadcast workflow into a structure that can be used over the long term.
uCg.positioning.desc2: In production environments that rely on fixed layouts, frequent content updates, quick on-air changes, and safer broadcast operation, the key is not just creating attractive graphics, but whether they can run reliably in real broadcast workflows.
uCg.differences.title: How is it different from basic title tools?
uCg.differences.items.0.title: More than flat title cards
uCg.differences.items.0.desc: U-CG allows title elements to be processed in 3D, with bevels, textures, Z-axis control, and lighting behavior, making the graphics feel closer to program-level packaging rather than simple flat overlays.
uCg.differences.items.1.title: More than one-time design work
uCg.differences.items.1.desc: With a template-based workflow and dynamic replacement, existing layouts can be reused quickly across different content, reducing repeated design effort.
uCg.differences.items.2.title: More than design — ready for broadcast
uCg.differences.items.2.desc: U-CG supports Fill / Key output, simultaneous 4K / HD broadcast, and multiple playout methods, allowing graphic packaging to move directly into real on-air workflows.
uCg.differences.items.3.title: More than stand-alone operation
uCg.differences.items.3.desc: From single-station use to networked workflows, permission control, asset sharing, approval stages, and backup structures, the system can be configured for more structured operation.
uCg.customerProblems.title: If these situations sound familiar, U-CG becomes more valuable
uCg.customerProblems.items.0.title: The layout stays the same, but the content changes all the time
uCg.customerProblems.items.0.desc: News, sports, forums, courses, and corporate live productions often rely on the same visual package while text, images, or data need to be updated quickly.
uCg.customerProblems.items.1.title: Last-minute text changes during live output are painful
uCg.customerProblems.items.1.desc: When information changes on site, titles and data fields must be updated quickly. Without a structured workflow, the live pace slows down easily.
uCg.customerProblems.items.2.title: As scale grows, assets and process become harder to control
uCg.customerProblems.items.2.desc: As channels, users, templates, and playout lists increase, long-term maintenance becomes difficult without clear permission, approval, and resource management rules.
uCg.value.title: The value it brings in practice
uCg.value.desc: The value of a graphics packaging system is not only visual quality, but faster updates, safer output, and a workflow that stays consistent over time.
uCg.value.items.0.title: Faster content updates
uCg.value.items.0.desc: Template and dynamic replacement logic help accelerate updates for text, images, video, and other elements.
uCg.value.items.1.title: More reliable on-air output
uCg.value.items.1.desc: From Fill / Key output to broadcast workflow integration, on-screen graphics can enter formal production environments more reliably.
uCg.value.items.2.title: Long-term reusable templates
uCg.value.items.2.desc: Fixed packaging can be managed systematically, reducing rework and improving efficiency for recurring content production.
uCg.value.items.3.title: A clearer workflow
uCg.value.items.3.desc: From creation and submission to review and playout, the system supports clearer task separation and permission control.
uCg.capabilities.title: Core functional capabilities
uCg.capabilities.desc: U-CG is not only about graphics design. Its strengths also include output, updating, integration, and networked workflow control.
uCg.capabilities.items.0.title: 3D Graphics Rendering
uCg.capabilities.items.0.desc: Supports 3D title elements, textures, bevels, lighting behavior, and third-party model import for richer visual packaging.
uCg.capabilities.items.1.title: Timeline-Based Template Logic
uCg.capabilities.items.1.desc: Each title package can manage large numbers of elements and attached effects through a timeline, making complex graphics more controllable.
uCg.capabilities.items.2.title: 4K / HD Simultaneous Broadcast
uCg.capabilities.items.2.desc: Supports dual 4K / HD graphics workflows so one graphics package can serve multiple output requirements without duplicated work.
uCg.capabilities.items.3.title: Multiple Playout Modes
uCg.capabilities.items.3.desc: Supports scheduled playout, manual playout, controlled playout, and list-based playout, allowing the workflow to match different program environments.
uCg.capabilities.items.4.title: Third-Party Playlist Integration and Real-Time Updates
uCg.capabilities.items.4.desc: Supports third-party playlist input such as XML and allows on-air updates to entries, improving synchronization efficiency and accuracy.
uCg.capabilities.items.5.title: Networked Workflow, Permissions, and Review Stages
uCg.capabilities.items.5.desc: Can extend into a networked graphics system with asset sharing, channel isolation, permission levels, approval stages, backup logic, and monitoring.
uCg.useCases.title: What kinds of content is it suitable for?
uCg.useCases.items.0.title: News and Information Programs
uCg.useCases.items.0.desc: Suitable for title bars, information bars, breaking updates, program packaging, and fixed-layout update workflows.
uCg.useCases.items.1.title: Sports Coverage
uCg.useCases.items.1.desc: Suitable for scoreboards, roster graphics, match data, and fast-changing sports graphics packages.
uCg.useCases.items.2.title: Forums and Corporate Live Production
uCg.useCases.items.2.desc: Suitable for speaker information, agenda bars, visual themes, on-screen information, and multi-segment content switching.
uCg.useCases.items.3.title: Channel-Style and Long-Term Program Content
uCg.useCases.items.3.desc: Suitable for long-term content structures that need template management, multi-user collaboration, review stages, and networked playout.
uCg.integration.title: Compatible modules
uCg.integration.desc: U-CG is best used as part of a full production workflow, so graphics packaging is not only designed, but also delivered reliably into broadcast, live production, and program execution.
uCg.integration.items.0.desc: Brings title graphics, information bars, and on-screen packaging into the switching and playout workflow.
uCg.integration.items.1.desc: Adds complete graphics and information packaging on top of virtual scenes and real-time rendered output.
uCg.integration.items.2.desc: Helps organize different sources and output routes so Fill / Key and overall signal flow remain smoother.
uCg.integration.items.3.desc: Can connect to more formal automation and broadcast workflows through playlists, data exchange, and structured process logic.
uCg.implementation.title: Implementation process
uCg.implementation.items.0.title: Requirement Review
uCg.implementation.items.0.desc: Start by confirming content type, graphics style, playout method, and whether 4K / HD or networked structure is required.
uCg.implementation.items.1.title: Template and Workflow Setup
uCg.implementation.items.1.desc: Plan title templates, replacement fields, playout method, and whether third-party playlists or workflow logic should be connected.
uCg.implementation.items.2.title: Testing and Integration
uCg.implementation.items.2.desc: Confirm that output, title updates, template reuse, playlist input, and permission workflows operate reliably.
uCg.implementation.items.3.title: Delivery and Expansion
uCg.implementation.items.3.desc: After launch, the system can continue to expand according to program type, channel scale, and workflow structure.
uCg.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
uCg.faq.items.0.q: Is it only for simple subtitles?
uCg.faq.items.0.a: No. U-CG can handle information bars, scoreboards, program packaging, show graphics, and more advanced 3D-style graphics effects.
uCg.faq.items.1.a: Yes. With template-based logic and dynamic replacement, text, images, video, and other elements can be updated more quickly.
uCg.faq.items.2.q: Does it support simultaneous 4K and HD playout?
uCg.faq.items.2.a: Yes. U-CG supports dual 4K / HD graphics workflows to meet different output requirements.
uCg.faq.items.3.q: Can it connect with third-party broadcast systems?
uCg.faq.items.3.a: Yes. It can integrate through playlists and data exchange logic, and it also supports real-time updates during playout.
uCg.faq.items.4.q: Is it only available as a stand-alone system?
uCg.faq.items.4.a: Not necessarily. Depending on requirements, it can be configured from a single station to a networked workflow with asset sharing, permission levels, review stages, and backup structure.
uCg.cta.title: Want to see whether U-CG fits your graphics workflow?
uCg.cta.desc: If you are planning title graphics and on-screen packaging for news, sports, forums, corporate live production, or program content, we can help configure a more suitable system structure based on your output and workflow needs.
uTp.hero.title: U-TP
uTp.hero.desc: A teleprompter system that combines an embedded controller, intelligent prompting, remote control, and speaking workflow into one structure, built for environments that need more stable delivery, faster script updates, and a cleaner on-site setup.
uTp.positioning.title: More than scrolling text — a more complete prompting workflow
uTp.positioning.desc1: U-TP is not only about showing script text in front of the speaker. It brings together prompting hardware, control methods, script updates, and speaking rhythm into a more stable working process.
uTp.positioning.desc2: Compared with traditional methods that rely on an external computer and manual operation, U-TP focuses more on compact structure, fast startup, flexible control, and integration with a wider studio workflow.
uTp.differences.title: How is it different from a basic teleprompter?
uTp.differences.items.0.title: More than an external computer setup
uTp.differences.items.0.desc: U-TP uses an embedded hardware controller instead of a traditional external computer, making the structure more compact, faster to start, and easier to hide within the production environment.
uTp.differences.items.1.title: More than manual scrolling
uTp.differences.items.1.desc: It supports AI voice-based control, allowing prompt movement to better follow speaking speed and delivery rhythm with less manual intervention.
uTp.differences.items.2.title: More than one control method
uTp.differences.items.2.desc: In addition to intelligent control, it can also be operated by keyboard, mouse, remote controller, or foot pedal, making it easier to adapt to different production needs.
uTp.differences.items.3.title: More than a single-speaker setup
uTp.differences.items.3.desc: It supports multi-speaker synchronization, multilingual adaptation, remote control, and live script editing, making it more suitable for complex and recurring production environments.
uTp.customerProblems.title: If these situations sound familiar, U-TP becomes more valuable
uTp.customerProblems.items.0.title: The speaking rhythm often falls out of sync with the prompt
uTp.customerProblems.items.0.desc: When delivery speed changes, pauses happen, or lines are added on the fly, manual scrolling often struggles to stay aligned with the speaker.
uTp.customerProblems.items.1.title: The prompting setup depends too much on extra devices and manpower
uTp.customerProblems.items.1.desc: When the setup still requires an extra computer and dedicated operator, both the equipment chain and the workflow become more complicated and fragile.
uTp.customerProblems.items.2.title: Scripts often need last-minute edits, but the workflow is too slow
uTp.customerProblems.items.2.desc: When content must be updated in real time, shared by multiple users, or adjusted remotely, production efficiency drops quickly if the script cannot be edited and located fast enough.
uTp.value.title: The value it brings in practice
uTp.value.desc: The value of a teleprompter system is not only whether text can be displayed, but whether the speaker can deliver more naturally and the production team can keep the workflow more stable.
uTp.value.items.0.title: More natural speaking rhythm
uTp.value.items.0.desc: Prompting can stay closer to the actual pace and delivery style of the speaker, reducing the stress of chasing or waiting for lines.
uTp.value.items.1.title: A cleaner on-site setup
uTp.value.items.1.desc: The embedded controller reduces the need for external computers and supporting devices, making deployment and operation more stable.
uTp.value.items.2.title: Faster editing and positioning
uTp.value.items.2.desc: Scripts currently in preview or in use can be edited directly, and the correct section can be located quickly when changes are needed.
uTp.value.items.3.title: Easier integration into the wider production workflow
uTp.value.items.3.desc: Prompting no longer has to remain an isolated device and can work more naturally with studio control logic and program rhythm.
uTp.capabilities.title: Core functional capabilities
uTp.capabilities.desc: U-TP is not only about displaying text. Its strengths also cover hardware structure, control flexibility, script handling, and workflow integration.
uTp.capabilities.items.0.title: Embedded Controller Architecture
uTp.capabilities.items.0.desc: Uses an embedded hardware controller with a compact structure, fast startup, higher concealment, and reduced maintenance burden compared with a traditional computer-based setup.
uTp.capabilities.items.1.title: AI Voice-Based Control
uTp.capabilities.items.1.desc: Can recognize speaking rhythm and pace to keep prompting more closely aligned with actual delivery, reducing manual intervention.
uTp.capabilities.items.2.title: Multiple Traditional Control Methods
uTp.capabilities.items.2.desc: In addition to AI voice control, it also supports keyboard, mouse, remote control, and foot pedal operation for more practical flexibility.
uTp.capabilities.items.3.title: Remote Control and Remote Import
uTp.capabilities.items.3.desc: The teleprompter can be controlled remotely over a network with similar control permissions to direct on-site operation, and script content can also be imported remotely.
uTp.capabilities.items.4.title: Multi-Speaker and Multilingual Support
uTp.capabilities.items.4.desc: Supports synchronized display for multiple speakers with color-based distinction and can adapt to multilingual content environments.
uTp.capabilities.items.5.title: Live Script Editing and Auto-Reading of Latest Files
uTp.capabilities.items.5.desc: Current scripts can be edited directly during preview or use, and the system can also automatically read the latest file from the designated directory.
uTp.useCases.title: What kinds of content is it suitable for?
uTp.useCases.items.0.title: News Delivery and Host Presentation
uTp.useCases.items.0.desc: Suitable for anchors, hosts, and studio delivery scenarios that require stable speaking rhythm and long-form script reading.
uTp.useCases.items.1.title: Forums, Interviews, and Program Recording
uTp.useCases.items.1.desc: Suitable for host-led transitions, segment changes, and production scenarios where multiple speakers take turns delivering lines.
uTp.useCases.items.2.title: Online Courses and Educational Content
uTp.useCases.items.2.desc: Suitable for instructor-led recording, helping educational delivery stay more stable and natural.
uTp.useCases.items.3.title: Corporate Presentations and Spoken Explanation Content
uTp.useCases.items.3.desc: Suitable for business-facing spoken content that relies on scripted explanation while maintaining natural eye contact and fewer delivery errors.
uTp.integration.title: Compatible modules
uTp.integration.desc: U-TP works well inside a wider production workflow so speaker delivery, output control, and program rhythm can stay aligned rather than operating independently.
uTp.integration.items.0.desc: Helps keep prompting rhythm aligned with switching, segment flow, and the broader broadcast logic.
uTp.integration.items.1.desc: When on-screen information and spoken delivery need to stay synchronized, prompting can work more smoothly with the graphics rhythm.
uTp.integration.items.2.desc: Supports more natural eye contact and steadier delivery in virtual studio and high-spec production environments.
uTp.integration.items.3.desc: Can function as a controlled device track within timeline-based logic so prompting content follows the structure of the show more closely.
uTp.implementation.title: Implementation process
uTp.implementation.items.0.title: Requirement Review
uTp.implementation.items.0.desc: Start by confirming the use case, number of speakers, language needs, control method, and whether remote collaboration is required.
uTp.implementation.items.1.title: Prompting Workflow Setup
uTp.implementation.items.1.desc: Configure AI voice control, traditional control methods, script import logic, and multi-speaker display behavior according to the requirement.
uTp.implementation.items.2.title: Testing and Integration
uTp.implementation.items.2.desc: Verify prompt speed, remote control, script updates, and on-site rhythm matching before launch.
uTp.implementation.items.3.title: Delivery and Expansion
uTp.implementation.items.3.desc: After launch, the setup can continue to evolve according to show format, speaker arrangement, and studio workflow needs.
uTp.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
uTp.faq.items.0.q: Does it always require a dedicated operator?
uTp.faq.items.0.a: Not necessarily. U-TP supports AI voice-based control to reduce dependence on manual operation, while also keeping traditional control methods as flexible options.
uTp.faq.items.1.q: Can scripts be edited while in use?
uTp.faq.items.1.a: Yes. Script content currently in preview or in use can be edited directly for on-the-fly adjustments.
uTp.faq.items.2.a: Yes. The teleprompter can be controlled remotely over the network, and content can also be imported remotely.
uTp.faq.items.3.q: Does it support multiple languages and multiple speakers?
uTp.faq.items.3.a: Yes. It supports multilingual content adaptation and synchronized multi-speaker display with color-based distinction.
uTp.faq.items.4.q: Is it better suited for a single presenter, or also for show-style workflows?
uTp.faq.items.4.a: Both are possible. It can be configured for a single speaking workflow or integrated into a broader studio and program control process.
uTp.cta.title: Want to see whether U-TP fits your prompting workflow?
uTp.cta.desc: If you are planning news delivery, forums, interviews, course recording, business presentations, or other spoken-format content, we can help configure a more suitable prompting structure based on the actual use case and control needs.
uHub.hero.title: U-Hub
uHub.hero.desc: A three-way encoding, decoding, and signal bridge module that brings 12G-SDI, NDI HX, and HDMI signal paths into one device, built for environments that need high image quality, low latency, synchronization, and practical deployment.
uHub.positioning.title: More than a converter — a bridge node inside the signal workflow
uHub.positioning.desc1: The value of U-Hub is not limited to format conversion. It creates a practical bridge path between different devices, signal standards, and workflow structures.
uHub.positioning.desc2: When SDI, HDMI, and NDI coexist in the same environment, the real issue is not whether a cable can be connected, but whether the whole path can enter, exit, synchronize, and preserve the expected image quality and latency performance.
uHub.differences.title: How is it different from a basic converter?
uHub.differences.items.0.title: More than one-way conversion
uHub.differences.items.0.desc: U-Hub supports multi-direction conversion between 12G-SDI, NDI HX, and HDMI, allowing it to work as an encoder, decoder, or intermediate bridge node depending on the actual need.
uHub.differences.items.1.title: More than only encoding or only decoding
uHub.differences.items.1.desc: Its integrated encode/decode design allows one device to take on multiple on-site roles, improving deployment flexibility and reducing planning complexity.
uHub.differences.items.2.title: More than just getting a picture on screen
uHub.differences.items.2.desc: Beyond signal conversion, it emphasizes 4Kp60, YCbCr 4:2:2, high-quality encoding and decoding, low latency, and audio-video synchronization for more formal production and display workflows.
uHub.differences.items.3.title: More than a desktop accessory — a deployable system component
uHub.differences.items.3.desc: With a compact body and plug-and-play deployment, it can be integrated into existing systems, temporary projects, or gradual IP transition structures as a stable bridge component.
uHub.customerProblems.title: If these situations sound familiar, U-Hub becomes more valuable
uHub.customerProblems.items.0.title: Mixed formats make the signal path difficult to organize
uHub.customerProblems.items.0.desc: The source may be SDI, the display may be HDMI, and the transport path may need to run on NDI. Without a bridge node, the overall system quickly becomes fragmented and harder to maintain.
uHub.customerProblems.items.1.title: The issue is not whether conversion works, but whether latency and sync stay under control
uHub.customerProblems.items.1.desc: In real workflows, the harder part is often not whether a signal appears, but whether latency, audio-video mismatch, or image quality loss become problems during use.
uHub.customerProblems.items.2.title: The system needs to move toward IP, but cannot be rebuilt all at once
uHub.customerProblems.items.2.desc: When existing equipment is still largely SDI or HDMI-based but the next workflow step requires NDI, a mixed structure often needs a practical transition and bridging solution.
uHub.value.title: The value it brings in practice
uHub.value.desc: The value of a signal bridge module is not only making formats talk to each other, but making the whole workflow easier to deploy, easier to preserve image quality, and easier to control in terms of latency and synchronization.
uHub.value.items.0.title: A clearer signal path
uHub.value.items.0.desc: It makes the relationships between SDI, HDMI, and NDI inputs and outputs easier to plan and maintain, reducing temporary improvised wiring decisions.
uHub.value.items.1.title: More flexible deployment
uHub.value.items.1.desc: Its integrated encode/decode design allows the same unit to switch roles across different projects, whether for fixed systems, temporary events, or transitional structures.
uHub.value.items.2.title: More controllable image quality and latency
uHub.value.items.2.desc: With 4Kp60, 4:2:2 processing, and low-latency transmission, it is better suited to environments with stricter visual and response requirements.
uHub.value.items.3.title: Easier integration into IP-based workflows
uHub.value.items.3.desc: It fits well into transitional or mixed structures, helping existing equipment move step by step into a broader IP-based production workflow.
uHub.capabilities.title: Core functional capabilities
uHub.capabilities.desc: The focus of U-Hub is not just conversion itself, but building a signal bridge capability that can be used in formal workflows.
uHub.capabilities.items.0.title: SDI / NDI / HDMI Three-Way Conversion
uHub.capabilities.items.0.desc: Supports multi-direction conversion between 12G-SDI, NDI HX, and HDMI, allowing flexible configuration according to source and output needs.
uHub.capabilities.items.1.title: Integrated Encode and Decode
uHub.capabilities.items.1.desc: A single unit can cover both encoding and decoding roles, reducing dependence on separate direction-specific devices in system planning.
uHub.capabilities.items.2.title: 4Kp60 UHD Support
uHub.capabilities.items.2.desc: Both encoding and decoding can support up to 4Kp60 UHD resolution, making it suitable for high-quality video workflows.
uHub.capabilities.items.3.title: 4:2:2 High-Quality Encoding and Decoding
uHub.capabilities.items.3.desc: Uses YCbCr 4:2:2 processing to balance image quality requirements with the practical needs of professional production environments.
uHub.capabilities.items.4.title: Low Latency and Synchronization
uHub.capabilities.items.4.desc: End-to-end latency can be kept below 100ms, while precise clocking and algorithms help maintain frame sync and audio-video sync.
uHub.capabilities.items.5.title: Compact Size and Plug-and-Play Deployment
uHub.capabilities.items.5.desc: Its small footprint and easy deployment make it suitable for racks, studio workflows, meeting environments, and hybrid system structures.
uHub.useCases.title: What kinds of scenarios is it suitable for?
uHub.useCases.items.0.title: IP-Based Production Workflows
uHub.useCases.items.0.desc: Suitable as a bridge node between SDI, HDMI, and NDI in mixed environments, helping production move step by step toward IP-based structures.
uHub.useCases.items.1.title: Low-Latency Display and Transmission
uHub.useCases.items.1.desc: Suitable for live environments that require lower-latency monitoring, preview, or cross-end transmission.
uHub.useCases.items.2.title: Video Conference Input and Output
uHub.useCases.items.2.desc: Suitable for meeting systems and remote collaboration environments that need to integrate different signal formats.
uHub.useCases.items.3.title: Studio and Live Event Mixed Systems
uHub.useCases.items.3.desc: Suitable for studios, venues, or temporary production projects where existing equipment formats are inconsistent but still need stable integration.
uHub.integration.title: Compatible modules
uHub.integration.desc: U-Hub works best inside a larger system, so signals are not only converted, but can move more smoothly into virtual production, playout control, and display output workflows.
uHub.integration.items.0.desc: Helps establish clearer input and output paths between different sources and virtual production workflows.
uHub.integration.items.1.desc: Makes it easier to organize the relationship between external sources, IP signals, and playout control systems.
uHub.integration.items.2.desc: Provides more flexible bridge thinking between graphics workflows, Fill / Key structures, and other signal paths.
uHub.integration.items.3.desc: Works well as an integration component between source devices, displays, and network transport paths where standards do not naturally match.
uHub.implementation.title: Implementation process
uHub.implementation.items.0.title: Requirement Review
uHub.implementation.items.0.desc: Start by confirming source formats, output formats, whether NDI is needed, resolution requirements, and acceptable latency range.
uHub.implementation.items.1.title: Signal Path Planning
uHub.implementation.items.1.desc: Plan the encoding, decoding, or bridge role between SDI, HDMI, and NDI according to the actual workflow.
uHub.implementation.items.2.title: Testing and Integration
uHub.implementation.items.2.desc: Confirm whether image quality, latency, audio-video sync, display behavior, and overall path stability meet on-site requirements.
uHub.implementation.items.3.title: Delivery and Expansion
uHub.implementation.items.3.desc: After deployment, the setup can continue to evolve according to future system expansion and the progress of IP-based integration.
uHub.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
uHub.faq.items.0.q: Is it an encoder or a decoder?
uHub.faq.items.0.a: It can serve both roles. U-Hub uses an integrated encode/decode design and can work as an encoder, decoder, or bridge depending on the actual scenario.
uHub.faq.items.1.a: Yes. Both encoding and decoding can support up to 4Kp60 UHD workflows.
uHub.faq.items.2.a: U-Hub is designed for low-latency transmission, with end-to-end latency below 100ms while still maintaining audio-video synchronization.
uHub.faq.items.3.q: Is it suitable for meeting rooms or business applications?
uHub.faq.items.3.a: Yes. In addition to production workflows, it also fits video conference input/output and lower-latency display requirements.
uHub.faq.items.4.q: Is it better for new systems or as a transition tool for existing systems?
uHub.faq.items.4.a: Both. It can work in newly built systems and is also very suitable as a transition bridge for existing SDI / HDMI environments that need to move gradually toward NDI workflows.
uHub.cta.title: Want to see whether U-Hub fits your signal workflow?
uHub.cta.desc: If you are planning IP-based production, low-latency transmission, meeting system integration, or mixed studio signal structures, we can help configure a more suitable bridge path based on your source, output, and latency requirements.
uGemini.hero.title: U-Gemini
uGemini.hero.desc: A content production system that builds a reusable digital twin from real-person footage and drives lip sync through audio input, built for environments that need a fixed identity, stable spoken delivery, and long-term repeated output.
uGemini.positioning.title: Not about generating a new character, but extending the use of an existing identity
uGemini.positioning.desc1: The core of U-Gemini is not creating a virtual person from scratch. It uses real-person footage to build a reusable digital twin while preserving visual identity, on-camera style, and recognition.
uGemini.positioning.desc2: For environments that need a fixed role, stable spoken delivery, and large-scale repeated output, the key is not whether a flashy effect can be made once, but whether an existing person can become a reusable content asset.
uGemini.differences.title: How is it different from general digital human services?
uGemini.differences.items.0.title: It does not create a character from scratch
uGemini.differences.items.0.desc: U-Gemini is based on real-person footage. Its focus is the duplication and extension of an existing identity, not the creation of a brand-new character.
uGemini.differences.items.1.title: It is not just for making one video
uGemini.differences.items.1.desc: Once established, the same digital twin can continue to be used across different audio tracks, versions, and content topics.
uGemini.differences.items.2.title: It is not built around a cloud-only service model
uGemini.differences.items.2.desc: The direction is better suited to local deployment and owned usage, focusing on long-term content assets rather than one-time cloud generation.
uGemini.differences.items.3.title: It is not just a short-video feature, but something that can connect to production workflows
uGemini.differences.items.3.desc: U-Gemini is better suited to work with virtual sets, playout control, and program workflows so the digital twin can enter more formal production environments.
uGemini.customerProblems.title: If these situations sound familiar, U-Gemini becomes more valuable
uGemini.customerProblems.items.0.title: A fixed on-camera role is difficult to schedule over the long term
uGemini.customerProblems.items.0.desc: When content depends on a consistent presenter, brand representative, or speaker image, scheduling, reshoot costs, and long-term coordination quickly become practical issues.
uGemini.customerProblems.items.1.title: Repeating shoots with the same identity is inefficient
uGemini.customerProblems.items.1.desc: When content topics keep changing but the on-camera identity needs to remain the same, repeated filming increases both time cost and production pressure.
uGemini.customerProblems.items.2.title: There is a need for long-term content assets, but no reusable character mechanism
uGemini.customerProblems.items.2.desc: If every project still depends on fresh shooting and repeated staffing, a fixed identity never truly becomes a reusable content asset.
uGemini.value.title: The value it brings in practice
uGemini.value.desc: The value of a digital twin system is not only that the image can move, but that an existing role can be used more steadily and more continuously in content production.
uGemini.value.items.0.title: A fixed identity can be reused over the long term
uGemini.value.items.0.desc: Once established, the same role can continue to appear across different content topics while maintaining a consistent presence.
uGemini.value.items.1.title: Spoken content becomes easier to scale
uGemini.value.items.1.desc: By turning real-person footage into a reusable digital twin, recurring speaking-based content can be produced more efficiently.
uGemini.value.items.2.title: Better suited for owned content assets
uGemini.value.items.2.desc: A local deployment and long-term usage model makes it easier to keep identity, source material, and output workflow inside an owned system.
uGemini.value.items.3.title: Easier to enter formal production workflows
uGemini.value.items.3.desc: It can work with virtual sets, show control, and graphics modules, so the digital twin is not limited to a single video but can enter broader production workflows.
uGemini.capabilities.title: Core functional capabilities
uGemini.capabilities.desc: The core capability of U-Gemini is the use of real-person footage, audio-driven lip sync, and repeatable long-term use, not character generation from scratch.
uGemini.capabilities.items.0.title: Building a Digital Twin from Real Footage
uGemini.capabilities.items.0.desc: Uses real-person video footage as the foundation to build a reusable digital twin while preserving identity and continuity.
uGemini.capabilities.items.1.title: Audio-Driven Lip Sync
uGemini.capabilities.items.1.desc: Drives mouth movement through audio or voice input so the speaking image aligns more closely with the sound content for practical output use.
uGemini.capabilities.items.2.title: Reuse of a Fixed Role Across Content
uGemini.capabilities.items.2.desc: Once built, the same role can continue to be used across different topics, versions, and segments, reducing repeated filming needs.
uGemini.capabilities.items.3.title: Local Deployment Direction
uGemini.capabilities.items.3.desc: The system is better suited to locally managed materials, workflows, and outputs, supporting long-term use without depending on cloud usage limits.
uGemini.capabilities.items.4.title: Multi-Version and Multi-Language Extension
uGemini.capabilities.items.4.desc: Once a fixed role is established, it can be extended with different audio content into multiple output versions for different language or market needs.
uGemini.capabilities.items.5.title: Works with Virtual Sets and Show Workflows
uGemini.capabilities.items.5.desc: The digital twin can be output independently or combined with virtual sets, playout control, and graphics modules to support fuller program production.
uGemini.useCases.title: What kinds of content is it suitable for?
uGemini.useCases.items.0.title: Corporate Presentation and Brand Delivery
uGemini.useCases.items.0.desc: Suitable for business-facing applications that rely on a consistent brand role, explanatory content, and a stable on-camera presence.
uGemini.useCases.items.1.title: Education and Course Content
uGemini.useCases.items.1.desc: Suitable for teaching environments that need repeated output, a stable presenter image, and long-term content accumulation.
uGemini.useCases.items.2.title: Fixed Host or Presenter Roles
uGemini.useCases.items.2.desc: Suitable for program, column, or brand content that depends on a stable role setting and long-term extension of the same identity.
uGemini.useCases.items.3.title: Multi-Version and Multi-Language Content Production
uGemini.useCases.items.3.desc: Suitable for keeping the same identity while extending the output into different audio versions and target markets.
uGemini.integration.title: Compatible modules
uGemini.integration.desc: U-Gemini works best inside a broader production structure so the digital twin is not only output alone, but can actually enter picture creation, playout, and show workflows.
uGemini.integration.items.0.desc: The digital twin can be integrated into virtual scenes and show images to form a more complete virtual production workflow.
uGemini.integration.items.1.desc: Digital twin content can be brought into show playout control, segment switching, and broader content workflows.
uGemini.integration.items.2.desc: When combined with graphics, information bars, and on-screen packaging, it can support a more complete program-level visual presentation.
uGemini.integration.items.3.desc: Suitable for integration into owned materials, audio assets, and local output workflows, helping form a long-term content asset structure.
uGemini.implementation.title: Implementation process
uGemini.implementation.items.0.title: Material Review
uGemini.implementation.items.0.desc: Start by confirming the quality of real-person footage, the intended use, the speaking format, and the expected output direction.
uGemini.implementation.items.1.title: Digital Twin Setup
uGemini.implementation.items.1.desc: Use real-person footage to build a reusable digital twin that serves as the fixed role for future content output.
uGemini.implementation.items.2.title: Audio-Driven Testing and Content Validation
uGemini.implementation.items.2.desc: Confirm whether audio-driven lip sync, visual output, and integration with the existing production workflow meet the requirement.
uGemini.implementation.items.3.title: Delivery and Ongoing Extension
uGemini.implementation.items.3.desc: After implementation, output methods can continue to expand according to future topics, language versions, and program needs.
uGemini.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
uGemini.faq.items.0.q: Does it generate a completely new virtual person from scratch?
uGemini.faq.items.0.a: No. The core of U-Gemini is to build a digital twin from real-person footage and extend the use of an existing identity.
uGemini.faq.items.1.q: Does it require real-person source material first?
uGemini.faq.items.1.a: Yes. The system builds the digital twin from real-person footage, so material quality and capture conditions directly affect the output result.
uGemini.faq.items.2.q: Is the core about text generation or audio-driven output?
uGemini.faq.items.2.a: The core is audio-driven lip sync, allowing an established identity to continue producing output with different sound content.
uGemini.faq.items.3.q: Is it better for one-time projects or long-term content?
uGemini.faq.items.3.a: Both are possible, but it is especially suited to long-term content, fixed-role use, and repeated output, where the value of a digital twin becomes clearer.
uGemini.faq.items.4.q: Can it work with virtual sets and broader show workflows?
uGemini.faq.items.4.a: Yes. U-Gemini can integrate with virtual scenes, playout control, and graphics modules to support more complete production workflows.
uGemini.cta.title: Want to see whether U-Gemini fits your content workflow?
uGemini.cta.desc: If you are planning fixed-role spoken delivery, educational content, brand explanation, business presentations, or long-term content output, we can help evaluate a more suitable implementation structure based on your material conditions and production method.
